name: Mixed Berry Protein Cheesecake
description: Mixed-berry flavor of the vanilla protein cheesecake base - a sugar-free mixed-berry swirl marbled through the batter, a slight imitation-blueberry note carried through the custard itself, and a berry scatter on top. Baked in the 300F water bath (~40-45 min) - now the standard across the cheesecakes; it's where the smooth, less-cracked top came from. Berries/extract are near-zero fat, so it stays close to the base (~140 cal/slice, ratio 9.7).

payload.instructionText: Make the swirl: simmer the mixed berries with the Splenda and cornstarch over medium heat, mashing, until jammy and thick (4-6 min). Cool fully before swirling - hot jam melts streaks into the batter.
Once fully chilled, top with a scatter of fresh or thawed mixed berries just before serving.

payload.text: The cornstarch keeps the swirl from weeping into the custard. Toss the topping berries in a pinch of cornstarch if they're wet.

The imitation blueberry extract goes into the batter, not the swirl - 0.5 tsp dispersed through the whole custard gives a slight blueberry note in every bite, while the swirl stays a pure mixed-berry ribbon. It's potent, so keep it at 0.5 tsp; more reads artificial. Add it with the wet ingredients before the flour so it distributes evenly.

payload.instructionText: Beat the light cream cheese alone until smooth, then add yogurt, blended cottage cheese, protein powder, sweetener, vanilla, lemon, the imitation blueberry extract, and vanilla pudding mix (or its no-pudding swap - see note). Sift/whisk in the AP flour smooth (no lumps) and mix just until combined — don't overwork it. Warm the bowl ~15 sec if it clumps.

Pour about two-thirds of the batter into the parchment-lined 9-inch pan. Spoon dollops of the cooled berry swirl over it, add the rest of the batter, dollop a little more swirl on top, and drag a knife through in S-curves to marble - 3-4 passes only (over-swirling greys the batter). Tap on the counter to level and pop bubbles.

payload.text: Swap: unsweetened applesauce works 1:1 for the pumpkin (75 g) as the fat-replacing moisture/body. Reduce it the same way (cook it down until thickened) so it doesn't loosen the batter - applesauce starts wetter and, with more sugar than pumpkin, browns and scorches faster, so keep it moving and pull it a touch sooner. It reads a little sweeter and more neutral - actually cleaner under a berry swirl, where pumpkin's faint earthiness can muddy things. Macro-trivial (~+6 cal/recipe). Recipe of record keeps pumpkin; this is the in-a-pinch / flavor-preference swap.

Out of vanilla SF pudding? Don't reach for a flavored pudding (butterscotch, etc.) - its flavor tints the whole cheesecake and fights the berry. And you barely need it for structure: the 2 tbsp AP flour plus the eggs already do the set, so the pudding is mostly vanilla flavor, a little sweetness, and the creamy body its gums lend. Clean swap: ~1 tbsp cornstarch (~8 g; or skip it and lean on the flour) + an extra splash of vanilla (~1/4-1/2 tsp) and a pinch more Splenda to cover what the pudding carried. For a berry cheesecake cornstarch is actually the cleaner pick - flavorless under the swirl. Macro-trivial; recipe of record keeps the pudding.
